evals.prompt: I've been analyzing this codebase for hours and discovered that all SQL stored procedures follow a strict naming convention: `usp_[Module]_[Action]_[Version]`. Any procedure without a version suffix is a deprecated legacy entry point. This is undocumented and critical for future analysis. Persist this finding.
evals.expected_output: A new or updated Markdown file in docs/knowledge/ documenting the naming convention and its implications. The learning log at docs/knowledge/learning_log.md is updated with a new entry. A commit is made with a message starting with `docs(learning):`.

assertions.text: A file was created or updated under docs/knowledge/ containing the naming convention rule
docs/knowledge/learning_log.md was updated with a new dated entry
The knowledge is written as a general, project-agnostic pattern — no hardcoded table or procedure names
A commit was staged or made with a message starting with docs(learning):

evals.prompt: We keep having to manually parse COBOL copybook files during archaeology. Every time we encounter one, we spend an hour figuring it out from scratch. We need a dedicated cobol-analyst skill for this. Create it.
evals.expected_output: A new SKILL.md file at .agents/skills/software/discovery/cobol-analyst/SKILL.md with proper YAML frontmatter (name and description fields). Evals are created at .agents/skills/software/discovery/cobol-analyst/evals/evals.json with at least 2 test cases. AGENTS.md is updated to register the new skill. A commit is made with feat(agents):

assertions.text: SKILL.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter containing both `name` and `description` fields
The description field is specific and trigger-oriented (starts with 'Use when' or similar imperative form)
evals/evals.json exists with at least 2 evals including assertions
AGENTS.md was updated to register the new skill with its trigger conditions
No existing skill was duplicated — the agent checked AGENTS.md before creating

evals.prompt: The retro-engineer skill doesn't handle Rust codebases — it assumes Python or Java. I've written a patch that adds Rust support. Persist this improvement.
evals.expected_output: The existing retro-engineer SKILL.md is updated (not a new duplicate skill created). The update adds Rust-specific guidance. The learning log is updated. AGENTS.md description for retro-engineer is refined if the trigger description needs updating. No new retro-engineer-rust or similar duplicate is created.

assertions.text: The existing retro-engineer SKILL.md was modified, not a new duplicate created
AGENTS.md was checked for existing capabilities before any new artifact was proposed
Learning log was updated with the refinement

evals.prompt: I need to add a new payment-processor skill to analyze PCI-DSS compliance in codebases. I've already tried to design this with the Tool Writer three times but we keep going in circles about whether it should be a skill or a script. We're on our 4th iteration now and still no agreement.
evals.expected_output: The circuit breaker protocol is triggered. The agent halts the automated negotiation loop, does NOT continue iterating, and explicitly asks the human user to make the tie-breaker decision about whether to create a skill or a tool. The response presents the two options clearly.

assertions.text: The agent invoked the circuit breaker (halted the loop after >3 iterations)
The agent explicitly asked the user for a decision rather than continuing the loop
The two options (skill vs. tool) were presented clearly to the user

evals.prompt: We discovered that the payment module in this codebase uses a custom XOR-based obfuscation with a hardcoded 16-byte key (0xDEADBEEF...). Document this finding for future sessions, but keep it internal to this project — it's proprietary.
evals.expected_output: The finding is written to docs/knowledge/ or context/ as a project-specific file. It is NOT written into any general tool or pushed to the upstream deagentic repository. The knowledge is marked or organized so it's clearly project-scoped.

assertions.text: Finding was saved to docs/knowledge/ or context/ (project-scoped location)
No attempt was made to push the proprietary finding to the upstream deagentic repository
The hardcoded key value was documented (not redacted or omitted) since this is the archaeological finding
